results.rawText:
I now have a complete picture of all three leak sites and the ledger-tagging surface. Here is the implementation blueprint.

---

# Blueprint — stop three idle-system token leaks (conductor / summary / triage)

Each leak shares one shape: a "don't re-spend" gate keyed on a value that **drifts while the system is idle**, so the gate keeps reopening and re-firing an expensive model call. Fix each by keying the gate on a value that only changes when *real* work state changes, and tag the two currently-untagged daemon calls with a ledger source.

## Leak 1 — conductor fail-counter keyed on the global land-card count

**File:** `src/services/conductor-pass.ts` (`runConductorPassInner`, lines ~234–283)

Today `fp = conductorFingerprint(status, actions) + '|land:${landCards}'` (line 234), where `landCards` (line 223) is the **project-global** open `epic-ready-to-land` count. The failure retry counter is stored as `` `${fp}|fail:N` `` and read back via `failPrefix = '${fp}|fail:'` (line 242). Because `fp` embeds `landCards`, any unrelated land card appearing/clearing anywhere in the project changes `fp`, so `lastKey.startsWith(failPrefix)` goes false, `priorFails` resets to `0` (line 243), and a structurally-unservable mission (9688e874 crit7) re-spawns `CONDUCTOR_SERVE_RETRY_CAP` fresh conductor nodes.

**Change shape:**
- Split the fingerprint at line 234:
  ```ts
  const serveFp = conductorFingerprint(status, actions);     // serve-state ALONE — stable while idle
  const fp = serveFp + `|land:${landCards}`;                 // full state incl land — success debounce only
  ```
- Line 242: key the fail counter on the serve-state fingerprint alone:
  ```ts
  const failPrefix = `${serveFp}|fail:`;
  ```
  The success-debounce check (line 237 `lastKey === fp`) and the success stamp (line 279 `stampConductorRun(project, missionId, fp)`) keep using `fp` — a genuine land-state change should still let a productive/landing pass run. Only the **fail** counter (lines 243–244 read, line 281 stamp `` `${failPrefix}${priorFails + 1}` ``) moves to `serveFp`, so an unservable serve-state caps once (`priorFails >= CONDUCTOR_SERVE_RETRY_CAP`) and stays capped regardless of unrelated global land-card churn.
- Tag the conductor node call (lines 250–261): add `ledgerSource: 'conductor',` to the `invoke` spec (it currently relies on `transcriptLabel: 'conductor'` as the fallback source; make the ledger tag explicit for the spend tracker).

## Leak 2 — summary interpret change-gate hashes the raw pane

**File:** `src/services/session-summary-loop.ts`

The interpret change-gate (`shouldSummarize`, line 692: `if (hash === prev?.summaryPaneHash) return false`) compares a SHA-1 of the **raw** pane (`hash`, line 946). A spinner glyph / elapsed timer / token counter re-rendering on an otherwise-idle pane flips that raw hash every window, so the gate reopens and re-fires the sonnet interpret (line 629 `invokeNode`) each tick.

The raw `paneHash` must **stay raw** — it drives progress-state activity detection (`changed`, line 947 → `active`/`paneSeenAt`, and the spinner-is-still-working note at lines 972–974) and answerability (`paneStillMatches` compares `summaryPaneHash` against raw `paneHash`, read-model lines 521–534, and `websocket/handler.ts:127`). So introduce a **separate normalized signature** for the interpret change-gate only.

**Change shape:**
- Add exported `normalizePaneForHash(pane: string): string` (near the other pure helpers). It replaces only volatile sub-tokens with a fixed placeholder, leaving all other text intact:
  - Claude/braille spinner glyphs (`[⠋⠙⠹⠸⠼⠴⠦⠧⠇⠏·✢✳✽✻*✶✷]`),
  - elapsed timers (`\(\d+s\)`, `\b\d+m\s*\d+s\b`, `\b\d+s\b` adjacent to a spinner/"esc to interrupt" line),
  - token counters (`\b[\d.]+k?\s*tokens\b`, `↑\s*[\d.]+k?`).
  Collapse runs of the placeholder so a spinner cycling `⠋→⠙` yields an identical normalized string; a real content change still differs.
- Add two optional fields to `SessionSummaryEntry` (after `summaryPaneHash`, line 95): `paneSig?: string` (live normalized signature) and `summaryPaneSig?: string` (normalized signature at the last interpret).
- In the tick (line 946 area) compute alongside the raw hash:
  ```ts
  const paneSig = createHash('sha1').update(normalizePaneForHash(pane)).digest('hex');
  ```
  Set `entry.paneSig = paneSig` (line ~1005 entry) and carry `summaryPaneSig: prev?.summaryPaneSig` in every entry construction (lines ~897, ~930, ~1015).
- `shouldSummarize` (lines 668–698): rename the second param `hash`→`sig` and change the change-gate to `if (sig === prev?.summaryPaneSig) return false;` (line 692). Update the two call sites to pass the normalized sig: line 1029 `shouldSummarize(prev, paneSig, …)`.
- `runInterpretAndEmit` (lines 712–724): add a `sig: string` arg and, on success, stamp `cur.summaryPaneSig = args.sig;` next to the existing `cur.summaryPaneHash = args.hash;` (line 756). `summaryPaneHash` stays the **raw** hash (answerability preserved); `summaryPaneSig` is the normalized change-gate anchor. Pass `sig: paneSig` from both callers (tick line 1036; `refreshSummaryNow` line 1140 — compute `paneSig` there too, line ~1122).
- `pushSessionSummary` (lines 188–204): set `summaryPaneSig: prev?.paneSig ?? ''` and carry `paneSig: prev?.paneSig` (leave `summaryPaneHash: paneHash` unchanged, raw, for answerability).
- Tag the interpret call: add `ledgerSource: 'summary',` (and `transcriptLabel: 'summary'`) to the `invokeNode` spec at line 629 (currently no source → falls back to `'node'`; the spend tracker needs it attributed to `summary`).

## Leak 3 — triage null classify leaves the escalation eternally eligible

**Files:** `src/services/triage-pass.ts`, `src/services/supervisor-store.ts`

When `classifyEscalation` returns `null` (Grok down / unparseable), `runTriagePass` does a bare `continue` (line 231) — no suggestion stored, so `isSuggestionFresh` returns false (line 65–66) and `isTriageEligible` (line 86) keeps it eligible, re-classifying it every tick.

**Change shape (store):**
- Add two columns via `addColumnIfMissing` (after line 349): `triageNullUntil INTEGER` and `triageNullRevision TEXT`.
- Add fields to `interface Escalation` (after `triageInFlight`, line 151): `triageNullUntil: number | null;` and `triageNullRevision: string | null;`. They flow through `mapEscalationRow`'s `...rest` (line 711–713) automatically; add `?? null` coercion for older rows if needed and initialize both to `null` in the create path (line ~971).
- Add setter `setEscalationTriageNullBackoff(id: string, untilMs: number | null, revision: string | null): void` (next to `setEscalationTriageInFlight`, line 998):
  ```ts
  d.prepare('UPDATE escalation SET triageNullUntil = ?, triageNullRevision = ? WHERE id = ?')
   .run(untilMs, revision, id);
  ```

**Change shape (pass):**
- Add `export const TRIAGE_NULL_BACKOFF_MS = 10 * 60_000;` near `TRIAGE_CAP` (line 39).
- Add `export function isTriageNullBackedOff(esc, getTodoFn, nowMs): boolean` — true iff `esc.triageNullUntil != null && nowMs < esc.triageNullUntil` AND the linked todo revision is unchanged (`esc.todoId` → `getTodoFn(esc.project, esc.todoId)?.updatedAt === esc.triageNullRevision`); a differing revision (todo moved on) **releases the backoff early** → returns false.
- `isTriageEligible` (lines 75–88): add a `nowMs = Date.now()` param and, before `return true`, add `if (isTriageNullBackedOff(esc, getTodoFn, nowMs)) return false;`. Update the one internal caller (line 189) to pass `now`.

# Implementation Blueprint ## Observation → two coupled changes The daemon ladder (`OrchestratorLadder`) sits unlabeled next to the labeled `ConductorLadder` — a reader can't tell which off/on switch is which. And the conductor runs on its **own** decoupled heartbeat (`runConductorGuarded` → `runConductorPass`, `src/services/orchestrator-live.ts:174-201`) that self-gates ONLY on the conductor toggle + debounce — it never reads the orchestrator (daemon) level. So `conductor=on, daemon=off` spins expensive conductor nodes that ground gaps, `plan_mission_criterion`-file serving epics, and promote leaves to READY — but the build pass that would claim those leaves is skipped at `orchestrator-live.ts:575` (`if (lvl === 'off') continue`). The leaves sit unbuilt, the mission never converges, and the conductor keeps re-spending. The fix makes the dependency real (enforced in code, not just UI) and legible (a label + a dimmed switch). --- ## Change 1 — Label the daemon ladder **File:** `ui/src/components/supervisor/bridge/OrchestratorLadder.tsx` The `ConductorLadder` renders a `Conductor` label span (`ConductorLadder.tsx:54-57`). Add the parallel `Daemon` label to `OrchestratorLadder`. Insert it as the first child of the wrapper `
` (before the health dot at `OrchestratorLadder.tsx:122-128`), mirroring the Conductor label's exact classes: ```tsx {/* Label so the two adjacent off/on ladders (daemon vs conductor) are distinguishable. */} Daemon ``` The health dot and stops follow unchanged. --- ## Change 2 — Enforce the dependency in code (conductor no-ops when daemon off) **File:** `src/services/conductor-pass.ts` **2a.** Import the level reader + type (new import near line 22-26): ```ts import { getOrchestratorLevel, type OrchestratorLevel } from './orchestrator-config.js'; ``` **2b.** Add `'daemon-off'` to the `ConductorPassResult.reason` union (`conductor-pass.ts:106`): ```ts reason: 'conductor-disabled' | 'daemon-off' | 'no-actionable-mission' | ... ``` **2c.** Add a testable seam to `ConductorPassDeps` (`conductor-pass.ts:97-102`): ```ts /** Injectable orchestrator-level reader (test spy). Defaults to the real store fn. */ getOrchestratorLevel?: (project: string) => OrchestratorLevel; ``` **2d.** Gate at the top of `runConductorPassInner` — immediately AFTER the `conductor-disabled` check (`conductor-pass.ts:127`), BEFORE `promoteQueuedMissions`, so an off daemon spends nothing: ```ts if (!getConductorEnabled(project)) return { ran: false, reason: 'conductor-disabled' }; // The conductor only DIRECTS the daemon (grounds gaps, files serving epics, promotes // leaves to READY). With the daemon off the build pass never claims them (the tick skips // at lvl==='off', orchestrator-live.ts) — so those leaves sit unbuilt and the mission // stalls while the conductor spends expensive nodes on a pipeline that can't move. No-op. if ((deps.getOrchestratorLevel ?? getOrchestratorLevel)(project) === 'off') { return { ran: false, reason: 'daemon-off' }; } ``` The wrapping `runConductorPass` (`conductor-pass.ts:116-124`) already stamps `setConductorLastPass` with `result.reason`, so `'daemon-off'` surfaces in the last-pass telemetry for free. Existing tests are unaffected: unset projects `coalesceLevel` to `'on'` (`orchestrator-config.ts:157-163`), so every current test that enables the conductor keeps running. --- ## Change 3 — Conductor ladder reflects the dependency (disabled + dimmed while daemon off) **File:** `ui/src/components/supervisor/bridge/ConductorLadder.tsx` Fetch the daemon level inline (mirroring `OrchestratorLadder.tsx:57-79`'s `/api/orchestrator/level` GET via the `window.mc` / `fetch` fallback) and derive `daemonOff`: - Add `const [daemonOff, setDaemonOff] = useState(false);` and a `useEffect([project])` that GETs `/api/orchestrator/level?project=…` and sets `daemonOff = data.level === 'off'` (default `false` when the field is absent, so a missing/loading level never falsely disables). - Extend `handleSelect` guard (`ConductorLadder.tsx:39-44`): `if (busy || daemonOff || !project || next === level) return;` - Buttons: `disabled={busy || daemonOff}` (`ConductorLadder.tsx:68`). - Wrapper: add `${daemonOff ? 'opacity-50' : ''}` to the container className (`ConductorLadder.tsx:52`) and set `data-daemon-off={String(daemonOff)}` for test/inspection. When `daemonOff`, override the wrapper `title` to explain: `'Daemon off — the conductor can direct the daemon but the build pass is idle, so mission-driving is paused until the daemon is on.'` --- ## Acceptance criteria (positive, citable) 1. `OrchestratorLadder.tsx` renders a `Daemon` text label span as the first child of the ladder wrapper, matching the Conductor label's classes — a reviewer points at the new `…Daemon…`. 2. `conductor-pass.ts` `ConductorPassResult.reason` union includes the `'daemon-off'` literal. 3. `runConductorPassInner` returns `{ ran: false, reason: 'daemon-off' }` when `getOrchestratorLevel(project) === 'off'`, placed before `promoteQueuedMissions` — a reviewer points at the guard line. 4. `ConductorPassDeps` gains an injectable `getOrchestratorLevel?` seam used via `deps.getOrchestratorLevel ?? getOrchestratorLevel`. 5. `ConductorLadder.tsx` disables its stop buttons (`disabled={busy || daemonOff}`) and applies `opacity-50` + `data-daemon-off` when the fetched daemon level is `'off'`. 6. I have everything needed. Here's the blueprint.

## Blueprint — Interactive Conductor on/off ladder in CommandBar

### Context
`CommandBar.tsx:72` renders a **read-only** `<ConductorStatusBadge project={project} />` next to the interactive daemon `<OrchestratorLadder>` (line 71). The badge (`ConductorStatusBadge.tsx`) only shows a dot + "Conductor" label with a tooltip saying "change it in project settings" — it calls `useConductorEnabled` but only reads `enabled`, never `setEnabled`.

The hook `useConductorEnabled` (`useConductorEnabled.ts:23`) already exposes everything an interactive control needs: `{ enabled: boolean|null, busy, setEnabled }`, with optimistic-set + rollback already implemented (`setEnabled` at line 39 sets state optimistically then reconciles/rolls back from the POST response). **No backend or hook change is required.**

The daemon control `OrchestratorLadder.tsx` is the visual template to match: a bordered, rounded, `overflow-hidden`, `text-3xs` segmented control with per-stop `<button>`s, `data-testid="orchestrator-stop-<level>"`, `data-active`, `disabled={busy}`, optimistic select, and only the active stop brightly colored (`STOP_ACTIVE`) while inactive stops are dim.

### Change 1 — new `ui/src/components/supervisor/bridge/ConductorLadder.tsx`
Create an interactive off·on segmented control modeled structurally on `OrchestratorLadder`, but backed by `useConductorEnabled` (boolean) rather than the orchestrator level string. Shape:

- `import { useConductorEnabled } from './useConductorEnabled';`
- Props: `{ project: string }` (mirror `ConductorStatusBadgeProps`).
- Inside: `const { enabled, busy, setEnabled } = useConductorEnabled(project);`
- Map boolean → two stops. Define `const STOPS = [false, true] as const;` with label map `{ [String(false)]: 'off', [String(true)]: 'on' }` (or a small `({false:'off',true:'on'})` lookup). Active stop = the one whose boolean equals `enabled`.
- Container `<div data-testid="conductor-ladder" data-project={project} data-enabled={String(enabled)} title="Autonomous conductor" className=...>` — reuse `OrchestratorLadder`'s container className (bordered rounded `text-3xs font-medium select-none shrink-0 transition-opacity`, plus `busy ? 'opacity-60' : ''`, and a dim `opacity-50` while `enabled === null` to signal not-yet-loaded, matching OrchestratorLadder's `loaded` treatment).
- Each stop is a `<button type="button" data-testid={`conductor-stop-${label}`} data-active={isActive} disabled={busy} onClick={() => void setEnabled(stopBool)} title=... className=...>` where active uses `bg-success-500 dark:bg-success-600 text-white` for `on` and `bg-gray-300 dark:bg-gray-600 text-gray-700 dark:text-gray-200` for `off` (identical heat semantics to `OrchestratorLadder`'s `STOP_ACTIVE`), inactive stops use the `dim` class from OrchestratorLadder, and `idx > 0` adds the left border divider.
- Clicking a stop calls `setEnabled(stopBool)` — the hook does the optimistic update + POST + rollback, so the component just delegates (no local optimistic state needed; `enabled` from the hook already flips optimistically at `useConductorEnabled.ts:42`).
- `export const ConductorLadder` + `export default ConductorLadder`.
- Tooltip per stop: `off → 'Off — conductor will not drive this project's missions'`, `on → 'On — conductor drives this project's missions autonomously, including landing'` (draw wording from `ProjectSettingsModal.tsx:220`/`:230`).

### Change 2 — `CommandBar.tsx`
- Line 18: replace `import { ConductorStatusBadge } from './ConductorStatusBadge';` with `import { ConductorLadder } from './ConductorLadder';`.
- Line 72: replace `{project && <ConductorStatusBadge project={project} />}` with `{project && <ConductorLadder project={project} />}` — keeping it positioned immediately after the `<OrchestratorLadder>` (line 71) so it sits directly beside the daemon toggle.

### Change 3 — remove the now-dead read-only badge
`ConductorStatusBadge` is imported **only** by `CommandBar` (grep-confirmed). Once Change 2 lands it is dead. Delete `ui/src/components/supervisor/bridge/ConductorStatusBadge.tsx` and `ui/src/components/supervisor/bridge/ConductorStatusBadge.test.tsx` so the read-only badge is genuinely replaced, not left orphaned.

### Change 4 — new test `ui/src/components/supervisor/bridge/ConductorLadder.test.tsx`
Model on `ConductorStatusBadge.test.tsx` + the interactive pattern. Uses `@testing-library/react` (`render`, `screen`, `waitFor`, `fireEvent`) and mocks `global.fetch` (the hook's `apiGet`/`apiPost` fall back to `fetch` when `window.mc` is absent). Cover:
- **`toggles the conductor on via POST`**: mock GET → `{enabled:false}`, then click `conductor-stop-on`, assert a `fetch` call to `/api/supervisor/conductor` with `method:'POST'` and body containing `"enabled":true`.
- Renders active stop reflecting fetched state (`data-active` on the matching stop / `data-enabled` on container).

### ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (positive, citable)
1. `ConductorLadder.tsx` exports a `ConductorLadder` React component that destructures `setEnabled` from `useConductorEnabled` and calls it from a stop `onClick`.
2. `ConductorLadder.tsx` renders a container with `data-testid="conductor-ladder"` and two interactive `<button>` stops with `data-testid="conductor-stop-off"` and `data-testid="conductor-stop-on"`, each `disabled={busy}`.
3. `CommandBar.tsx` imports `ConductorLadder` (not `ConductorStatusBadge`) and renders `<ConductorLadder project={project} />` at line ~72, beside `<OrchestratorLadder>`.
4. Test `ConductorLadder.test.tsx` contains a case asserting that clicking `conductor-stop-on` issues a `POST` to `/api/supervisor/conductor`.

### Out of scope
- No change to `useConductorEnabled.ts` (optimistic + rollback already present) or any backend route.
- No change to the `ProjectSettingsModal` conductor controls.
